title: Favourite dub actor actress
wikitext:
[]: Favourite dub actor/actress?             Quote Post by Trowa_Ayanami » Tue Aug 23, 2005 5:05 am Before I start this off, let's face it: dubbing sucks. The Japanease are always spot on when it comes to lending their voices in anime, mainly in Evangelion. But, back to the topic. Despite the fact that people would not like the dubbing as much, is there anyone in the Evangelion series and movies whose acting you seemed to connect to in the same way as the subbed version did. Here a few: Spike Spencer - Shinji Ikari a lot of people have been given quite an amount of hate towards the acting of Spike Spencer in Eva and it's so hard for me to understand why that is so. He seemed to be the perfect choice for Shinji judging by the way he speaks and it progresses throughout the series. From the series, Spike's most shining moment as Shinji most definitely comes from episode 16, when Shinji get's sucked in the black shadow by Leliel. Spike hits the parts right on target from the franticness of trying to get out of the Entry Plug to his conversation with himself. But while Megumi Ogata's (sub Shinji) acting makes Spike's acting look crap, Spike still did a great job. He even said that Shinji was his most toughest job to do. Amanda Winn Lee - Rei Ayanami There's really nothing much to say about Amanda's performance as Rei. She does a good job being the voice of blue haired, crimson eyed girl, she seems to be following Megumi Hayashibara's (sub Rei) lines of how she acts as Rei. I really like the fact that Amanda's voice is more quieter throughout the series as it just seems right, as she really seems to connect with the character with a passion. Allison Keith - Misato Katsuragi Honestly, when I first heard her voice of Misato from the start, I did not have any high hopes for her at all. But I was wrong, as like Spike, Alison doesn't start on a high, but greatly progresses through out the series. Her most shining moment: Episode 15, her conversation with Kaji saying how much like her father he was. There was something about that moment that I felt so sorry for her with the way she spoke. Her acting is no where near the greatness of Kotono Mitsuishi (sub Misato) but she did do a decent job of it. Tristan MacAvery - Gendo Ikari Ah yes, Gendo Ikari, the biggest bastard in Eva. Tristan was the first person in dub that I thought was better than the sub (Fumihiko Tachiki) The voice was a dead giveaway that he was a cold, heartless, prick, but later on, there was something about him that made Gendo really cool, despite the fact of his persona of the character. It was just a damn shame that he didn't lend his voice in the Platinum Edition. Well, that's my two cents.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Aug 23, 2005 4:18 pm Before I start this off, let's face it: dubbing sucks. The Japanease are always spot on when it comes to lending their voices in anime, mainly in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Aug 23, 2005 4:35 pm Allison Keith hands down.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Aug 23, 2005 6:57 pm Are you all nuts or something? Allison Keith is horrid as Misato! She reads her lines about as well as a porn star would read them. The voices in the EVA dub run the gamut from poor to horrible, with Spike Spencer leading the way as the worst of them all. He makes Shinji seem absolutely detestable.

[]:             Quote Post by Trowa_Ayanami » Tue Aug 23, 2005 7:19 pm ^ Not a dub fan then? I know that the dub in Eva is bad, but c'mon, there has to be at least one or two people that pulled it off. And besides, Spike Spencer wasn't that bad, it's Tiffany Grant who's the worst.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Aug 23, 2005 7:54 pm Trowa_Ayanami wrote: ^ Not a dub fan then? I know that the dub in Eva is bad, but c'mon, there has to be at least one or two people that pulled it off. And besides, Spike Spencer wasn't that bad, it's Tiffany Grant who's the worst.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Aug 23, 2005 8:06 pm Oh joy sub v dub again. . . Can we stick to the topic please and not rehash this again.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Wed Aug 24, 2005 8:54 am Rei - Amanda Winn Lee's greatest achivement was voicing Konoko in Oni, IMO. Shinji - Spike Spencer might just be the reason so many people say that Shinji is autistic. Asuka - Don't even get me started on this one... Fuyutsuki - *sniffle* Misato - *sniffles again* The rest of the cast - *breaks down and cries out loud*

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Wed Aug 24, 2005 9:49 am thewayneiac wrote: That's right; I don't watch dubs under any circumstances.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Aug 24, 2005 10:53 am I must be the only one here that likes Tiffany Grant! Yeah, and Allison Keith..I guess these are my faves dub actresses..

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Wed Aug 24, 2005 10:55 am the_seventh_child wrote: I must be the only one here that likes Tiffany Grant! Yeah, and Allison Keith..I guess these are my faves dub actresses..

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Aug 24, 2005 11:40 am Hay Trowa is it just me or has this thread gone a bit of topic with all the dub hating being thrown around?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Wed Aug 24, 2005 12:13 pm DatDude wrote: Hay Trowa is it just me or has this thread gone a bit of topic with all the dub hating being thrown around?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Aug 24, 2005 1:19 pm One of the biggest problems I had with the dub was the selection of a male voice actor for Shinji. I'm sure Spike Spencer gave it his best, but the fact is Shinji's character called for that half octave increase that can only really be afforded by a special kind of voice actor. The full octave difference afforded by the regular female voice actors is clearly to much, but the character is unsuited to the deeper pitch of a male actor. Obviously it is difficult to find voice actors fitting such a niche. Megumi Ogata is clearly gifted in this respect. It would have been of immense benefit to the dub to find such an actor with that specific pitch in their voice. So much of Shinji character is contained in his voice. That peculiar tone being both immature and mature, both timid and strangely resolute. It's a unique performance, and I would argue, critical in carrying the character across. The full male pitch gives Shinji too much vocal maturity, and hence possibly less sympathy in the minds of the audience. A full female pitch would be too noticable and might make the audience somewhat dismissive of Shinji's maturity.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Wed Aug 24, 2005 1:42 pm thewayneiac wrote: Are you all nuts or something? Allison Keith is horrid as Misato! She reads her lines about as well as a porn star would read them.

[]:             Quote Post by Magami no D00M » Wed Aug 24, 2005 1:43 pm Asuka's dub voice would've been perfect if Grant could've managed to make her sound younger (At least younger then Misato, not that Alison helped much...)

[]:             Quote Post by Trowa_Ayanami » Wed Aug 24, 2005 2:10 pm Magami no D00M wrote: Asuka's dub voice would've been perfect if Grant could've managed to make her sound younger (At least younger then Misato, not that Alison helped much...)

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Wed Aug 24, 2005 6:06 pm i kinda liked tiffany grant in the role, truth be told, but she sounded a bit too old for 14. spike spencer got better with time, but his voice has distracting lilts and cracks. and i can't hear amanda winn lee without thinking of mimiru from .hack//SIGN, who seemed more fitting to her goofball personality. ah well, you haven't heard horrible until you've heard the dub voices for makoto in end of evangelion. it makes him sound like a cross between a complete amateur and a hick. thank god he's a semi-minor character.

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Fri Aug 26, 2005 3:11 pm loved spike spencer though his knowledge of eva is low (his comentary will show along with matt greenfield or w/e his awful knowledge) tiffany grant though she is very... stuck up like when you first meet Asuka she is a great actress. Rei... well lets face it Rei isnt hard to play, all u gata do is talk slow and emotionless. really anyone could have done it. gendo was fine, liked EoE when he was helpless. umm hyugga's voice changed a lot form the first episodes to the movie i just noticed when watching my plats. so thats a bad thing, though he sounded good in EoE unn Misato sounds a whole lot better in Japanese then english i think. and besides that i dont have much opinion on other people.

[]:             Quote Post by Trowa_Ayanami » Tue Sep 06, 2005 11:58 am thewayneiac wrote: There's also a third way that this dub wrecks things. Besides bad performances and voices that don't match the characters, there are also characters who were distorted by the dub. The prime example of this is Ritsuko. In Japanese she is a complex and tragic character who is just as much Gendo's victim as anyone else. In the dub she comes off as Cruella De Vil .
